commandments.title: I am the Lord your God; you shall not have strange gods before Me.

questions.text: Have I doubted or denied the teachings of the Catholic Church?
Have I practiced any religion besides Catholicism, or taken part in non-Christian worship?
Have I presumed on God's mercy, assuming I can sin freely because God will always forgive?
Have I neglected my prayer life? Do I need to pray more often?
Have I failed to stand up for my Catholic faith when it was challenged or mocked?
Have I participated in occult practices, such as fortune-telling, astrology, or superstition?
Have I failed to make God a priority in my life, putting other things before Him?
Have I despaired of God's mercy, believing my sins are too great to be forgiven?
Have I been slothful in my work, studies, or responsibilities?

commandments.title: You shall not take the name of the Lord your God in vain.

questions.text: Have I used God's name without love or respect?
Have I used foul or vulgar language?
Have I insulted God or spoken of sacred things irreverently?
Have I broken a promise or oath made before God?

commandments.title: Remember to keep holy the Lord's Day.

questions.text: Have I missed Mass on Sunday through my own fault?
Have I missed Mass on a Holy Day of Obligation?
Have I arrived at Mass late or left early without a serious reason?
Have I been inattentive or disrespectful during Mass?
Have I been too busy to rest and spend time with my family on Sunday?

commandments.title: Honor your father and your mother.

questions.text: Have I disobeyed or shown disrespect to my parents?
Have I neglected to care for my elderly relatives?
Have I neglected my family or failed to give them the time and attention they need?
Have I failed to raise my children in the Catholic faith?
Have I disobeyed any legitimate authority?
Have I failed to teach my children about human dignity and sexuality according to Catholic teaching?
Have I physically hurt anyone or wished harm upon them?
Have I had an abortion, or helped someone else obtain an abortion?
Have I used artificial contraception?
Have I abused drugs or alcohol?
Have I allowed myself to be controlled by anger, hatred, or a desire for revenge?
Have I given scandal — that is, knowingly led others into sin?
Have I broken a just law?
Have I contemplated or attempted suicide?
Have I participated in or supported euthanasia?
Have I committed the sin of gluttony — eating or drinking to excess?
Have I engaged in sexual activity outside of marriage?
Have I given in to lustful thoughts or desires?
Have I viewed pornography or other impure material?
Have I read erotic literature or sought out impure entertainment?
Have I engaged in impure or immoral acts?
Have I failed to honor and respect my husband or wife in our marriage?
Have I acted on disordered sexual desires?
Have I stolen anything, or kept something that does not belong to me?
Have I pirated movies, music, or software?
Have I been greedy, or failed to give generously to my church and to the poor?
Have I cheated on taxes, at work, or at school?
Have I been wasteful or careless with what God has given me?
Have I acted selfishly, refusing to share with those in need?

commandments.title: You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.

questions.text: Have I lied?
Have I gossiped about others or damaged their reputation?
Have I been insincere or two-faced in my dealings with others?
Have I revealed information that should have been kept confidential?
Have I failed to speak up when someone's character was being unjustly attacked?

commandments.title: You shall not covet your neighbor's wife.

questions.text: Have I been jealous of someone else's spouse or relationship?
Have I entertained impure thoughts or fantasies about someone other than my spouse?
Have I consumed media — movies, TV, music, social media — that leads me toward impure thoughts?

commandments.title: You shall not covet your neighbor's goods.

questions.text: Have I been jealous of what someone else has?
Have I been envious of others' talents, success, or blessings?
Have I let material desires control my heart rather than desiring God above all things?
Have I been prideful or vain?
Have I failed to fast on Ash Wednesday and Good Friday?
Have I failed to abstain from meat on Fridays of Lent and Ash Wednesday?
Have I failed to receive the Eucharist at least once during the Easter season?
Have I failed to fast for one hour before receiving the Eucharist?
Have I received the Eucharist in a state of mortal sin?
Have I intentionally withheld a serious sin during Confession?
Have I voted for candidates or policies that conflict with Catholic moral teaching?

prayer_before.title: Prayer Before Confession
prayer_before.text:
Come, Holy Spirit, into my soul. Enlighten my mind that I may know the sins I ought to confess, and grant me the grace to confess them fully, humbly, and with a contrite heart. Help me to firmly resolve not to commit them again.

O Blessed Virgin, Mother of my Redeemer, mirror of innocence and sanctity, and refuge of penitent sinners, intercede for me through the Passion of your Son, that I may obtain the grace to make a good confession.

All you blessed angels and saints of God, pray for me, a most miserable sinner, that I may repent from my evil ways, that my heart may henceforth be forever united with yours in eternal love. Amen.

act_of_contrition.title: Act of Contrition
act_of_contrition.text: O my God, I am heartily sorry for having offended Thee, and I detest all my sins because I dread the loss of heaven and the pains of hell; but most of all because they offend Thee, my God, who art all good and deserving of all my love. I firmly resolve, with the help of Thy grace, to confess my sins, to do penance, and to amend my life. Amen.

thanksgiving.title: Thanksgiving After Confession
thanksgiving.text: Almighty and merciful God, I thank You for the grace of this Sacrament. I thank You for Your forgiveness and the restoration of Your friendship. Help me, Lord, to avoid the sins I have confessed and strengthen me to live faithfully according to Your will. May the Blessed Virgin Mary and all the saints intercede for me. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.

how_to_confess.title: How to Go to Confession
steps: Examine your conscience using the questions in each section of this module.
Enter the confessional and make the Sign of the Cross. Say: "Bless me, Father, for I have sinned. It has been [time] since my last Confession."
Confess your sins to the priest. Be honest and complete, especially regarding serious (mortal) sins.
Listen to the priest's counsel and accept your penance.
Pray the Act of Contrition when the priest asks you to.
After receiving absolution, leave the confessional and complete your penance.
